artPrompt: The dramatic moment a single municipal form tears clean down its middle, one ragged half dissolving into cyan light and drifting paper scraps, the surviving half still gripped by a disembodied bureaucrat's hand thrust toward the viewer in steep foreshortening. A rubber stamp falls away, its blank impression smudged and illegible, cold fluorescent glare raking across torn fibers, rendered as a bold painted graphic-novel splash — strong silhouettes, heavy directional inking and dramatic shadow shapes, fully painted (not flat cel comic).
name: Form ½-B (Partial Filing)
text:
Choose target spell, die roll, or coin flip. It is now only half-processed. Round its effect down to half, then argue with the table about which half.
If you cannot make change for {½}, the spell remains pending until someone at the table produces an actual coin.
flavorText: Box 4 was left blank. Everything that follows from Box 4 is therefore also blank.
